name: Neutron lifetime: beam-vs-bottle tension
requirement: Any candidate framework should account for the persistent ~4 sigma discrepancy between the two methods of measuring the free neutron lifetime: 'bottle' experiments (count UCNs surviving in storage) give tau_n = 877.75 +/- 0.32 s; 'beam' experiments (count protons produced by beta decay) give tau_n = 887.7 +/- 2.2 s. The difference (about 10 s) is one of the most stubborn unresolved tensions in low-energy physics.

regime.description: Free neutron at rest; weak charged-current decay n -> p e nu_e.

current_bound.experiment: UCNtau (LANL) for bottle; NIST trap for beam
current_bound.reference: Gonzalez et al. (UCNtau), 'Improved Neutron Lifetime Measurement with UCNtau', PRL 127, 162501 (2021), arXiv:2106.10375; Yue et al., 'Improved Determination of the Neutron Lifetime', PRL 111, 222501 (2013)

what_it_excludes: Possible explanations include: (a) systematic error in either method (favored by most physicists), (b) BSM neutron decay channels (e.g. n -> chi gamma where chi is a dark fermion, proposed by Fornal-Grinstein 2018, ruled out by SNS-IIa neutron-star observations and direct laboratory searches), or (c) unaccounted nuclear physics in the proton-counting normalization. Any candidate framework that addresses this tension should specify which.

parameterization.name: Direct: tau_n. BSM scenarios add a partial decay rate Gamma_BSM that affects bottle (which measures total lifetime) and beam (which measures only beta-decay rate) differently.
parameterization.reference: Fornal and Grinstein, PRL 120, 191801 (2018), arXiv:1801.01124 (proposed dark-decay scenario, now strongly disfavored).
parameterization.summary: If BSM neutron decay exists with branching B, bottle tau_n^obs = (Gamma_beta + Gamma_BSM)^-1, beam tau_n^obs = Gamma_beta^-1. Difference enables BSM partial width.

procedural.predicted_observable: tau_n^bottle - tau_n^beam = Gamma_BSM * tau_n^beta^2 / (1 + Gamma_BSM tau_n^beta).
procedural.bound_in_parameterization: Required Gamma_BSM ~ 1% of total to explain tension. This level of dark-decay branching is excluded by neutron-star equation-of-state observations, leaving systematic-error explanations as most likely.
framework_inputs: If the framework introduces BSM neutron decay channels, compute the partial branching fraction.
If the framework modifies V_ud or G_F, compute the resulting shift in tau_n^beta.

primary_references.citation: Gonzalez et al. (UCNtau), 'Improved Neutron Lifetime Measurement with UCNtau', PRL 127, 162501 (2021)
Yue et al., 'Improved Determination of the Neutron Lifetime', PRL 111, 222501 (2013)
Fornal and Grinstein, 'Dark Matter Interpretation of the Neutron Decay Anomaly', PRL 120, 191801 (2018)
